prompt: Q1: How should per-check config (today an arbitrary toml::Value) cross the WIT boundary in v1?
choices: Pass as a JSON string (config-json); guest SDK deserializes with serde into the author's own config struct (recommended, pragmatic)
Model config as WIT records / a recursive variant (typed at the boundary, but forces a schema or dynamic-value modeling)
Generic typed-per-check config baked into the SDK macro (most ergonomic, most SDK complexity)

prompt: Q2: How should WASI imports that std Rust guests pull in (clocks/stdio) be handled?
choices: Host links a minimal wasmtime-wasi context with no preopens / no real FS, purely to satisfy imports (recommended; ergonomic std guests)
Require guests to be built to import no WASI at all (smaller host, more constrained authoring)

prompt: Q3: What should the default timeout mechanism be?
choices: Epoch-based wall-clock deadline by default; fuel available as an opt-in determinism knob (recommended)
Fuel by default (deterministic but tuning-sensitive, like the current prototype)

prompt: Q4: What file-access mechanism should v1 ship?
choices: Host-imported host-fs with a host-enforced allowlist only (recommended; integrates with SourceTree, supports virtual/base trees)
host-fs import PLUS WASI preopened read-only dirs (coarse + fine; more host surface and wasmtime-wasi FS dependency)

prompt: Q5: Remove the sandbox-v1 runtime immediately once the component path lands (T11), rather than keeping both tiers running in parallel during migration?
